questions.question: What is the key difference between readonly() and shallowReadonly()?
options: readonly() only works with ref, shallowReadonly() works with reactive
readonly() makes all nested properties immutable; shallowReadonly() only protects root properties
readonly() throws errors; shallowReadonly() silently fails
There is no practical difference between them

questions.question: When you mutate a property on a readonly() object in development, what happens?
options: A TypeError is thrown and execution stops
The mutation succeeds but the UI does not update
Vue logs a warning and the mutation fails silently
The mutation succeeds and the original source is also updated

questions.question: What is the primary use case for wrapping state in readonly() when using provide/inject?
options: To improve performance by skipping deep reactivity
To prevent child components from mutating the parent's provided state
To allow the child to create a local copy of the state
To serialize the state for localStorage persistence
